heading: Minimum number of licensed individuals
label: 8301 Minimum number of licensed individuals
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource section (U.S. Code)830146:22346:404–1(8)Section 8301 prescribes the minimum number of licensed individuals on board certain vessels based on the vessel’s size or propulsion, length of voyage by distance or time, or any combination of these factors.Subsection (a) applies to certain merchant and passenger carrying vessels of the United States, each of which must have a licensed master regardless of the factors listed above. Subsection (b) applies to offshore supply vessels. Subsection (c) exempts fishing or whaling vessels or yachts from these requirements.Subsection (d) permits the Secretary to suspend any part of this chapter during a declared national emergency or to increase the number of licensed individuals required by this chapter if required for safe operation of a vessel.

Amendments2010—Subsec. (b). amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “An offshore supply vessel on a voyage of less than 600 miles shall have a licensed mate. However, if the vessel is on a voyage of at least 600 miles, the vessel shall have 2 licensed mates. An offshore supply vessel of more than 200 gross tons as measured under , or an alternate tonnage measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under may not be operated without a licensed engineer.”Pub. children.text: Each of those vessels propelled by machinery or carrying passengers shall have a licensed master.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a vessel other than a mobile offshore drilling unit, if on a voyage of less than 400 miles from port of departure to port of final destination, the vessel shall have 2 licensed mates; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a mobile offshore drilling unit, the vessel shall have licensed individuals as provided by regulations prescribed by the Secretary under .section 8101 of this title

A vessel of at least 200 gross tons but less than 1,000 gross tons as measured under , or an alternate tonnage measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under and propelled by machinery shall have 2 licensed mates.section 14502 of this titlesection 14302 of this titlesection 14104 of this title

A vessel of at least 100 gross tons but less than 200 gross tons as measured under , or an alternate tonnage measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under and propelled by machinery shall have one licensed mate. However, if the vessel is on a voyage of more than 24 hours, it shall have 2 licensed mates.section 14502 of this titlesection 14302 of this titlesection 14104 of this title

A freight vessel or a passenger vessel of at least 300 gross tons as measured under , or an alternate tonnage measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under and propelled by machinery shall have a licensed engineer.section 14502 of this titlesection 14302 of this titlesection 14104 of this title

An offshore supply vessel of less than 500 gross tons as measured under or 6,000 gross tons as measured under on a voyage of less than 600 miles shall have a licensed mate. If the vessel is on a voyage of at least 600 miles, however, the vessel shall have 2 licensed mates.section 14502 of this titlesection 14302 of this title

An offshore supply vessel of at least 6,000 gross tons as measured under on a voyage of less than 600 miles shall have at least two licensed mates, provided the offshore supply vessel meets the requirements of section 8104(g)(2). An offshore supply vessel of at least 6,000 gross tons as measured under on a voyage of at least 600 miles shall have three licensed mates.section 14302 of this titlesection 14302 of this title

An offshore supply vessel of more than 200 gross tons as measured under , or an alternate tonnage measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under , may not be operated without a licensed engineer.section 14502 of this titlesection 14302 of this titlesection 14104 of this title

children.label: c
children.text: Subsection (a) of this section does not apply to a fishing or whaling vessel, a mobile offshore drilling unit when on location, or a yacht.
suspend any part of this chapter during a national emergency proclaimed by the President; and
increase the number of licensed individuals on a vessel to which this chapter applies if, in the Secretary’s judgment, the vessel is not sufficiently manned for safe operation.

children.label: e
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe the minimum number of licensed individuals for an oil spill response vessel.
